channel.title: SSbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SSbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Stephen and Ayesha Curry talk about the one habit that separates good business leaders from great ones
item.description: The Currys, co-founders of Plezi Hydration, discuss leading with purpose.

item.title: ‘People Here Do Not Consider Themselves Poor. They Consider Themselves Broke.’
item.description: Big parts of the Rio Grande Valley turned against the party in recent years. One candidate is trying to win it back, one quinceañera at a time.

item.title: Russian Officials Say Town’s Air Is Toxic, Days After Strike on Oil Refinery
item.description: Since Ukraine attacked the refinery in Tuapse, starting a huge fire, residents have reported drops of dark, oily toxins falling from the sky.

item.title: After a Hero’s Fall, What to Do With the Art That Honored Him?
item.description: Artists who created public depictions of the civil rights icon Cesar Chavez have had revisit their works after revelations emerged of Mr. Chavez’s sex...

item.title: Inflated AI claims are under fire—and the regulatory reckoning is coming
item.description: A top securities litigation partner at Baker McKenzie argues that history—from dot-com fraud to ESG greenwashing—tells us exactly where AI disclosure ...

item.title: Goldman tackles AI’s missing link: the ‘world model’ that every AI godfather is racing to figure out
item.description: The researchers who built modern AI say it's still missing something fundamental. Goldman Sachs explains what—and why the answer could reshape everyth...

item.title: Current price of oil as of April 23, 2026
item.description: When oil prices change, it affects your energy costs—and even the price of everyday items. Here’s why.

item.title: The Generals Who Are Now Running Iran
item.description: The killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei ushered in a new form of collective leadership in the country, with more power for the Revolutionary Guards.

item.title: $106 Billion Loan Reflects E.U.’s View That Peace in Ukraine Is Far Away
item.description: Unlike previous European assistance packages, this one is heavily weighted toward military spending, meant to put Ukraine on solid footing for a long ...

item.title: The Iran War just made the clean energy transition non-negotiable
item.description: I've spent 20 years making the economic case for clean energy. Hormuz just made the geopolitical case — at the cost of many lives and $126-a-barrel oi...

item.title: The Generals Who Are Now Running Iran
item.description: The killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei ushered in a new form of collective leadership in the country, with more power for the Revolutionary Guards.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump Threatens Iranian Boats He Says Are Mining the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump’s latest threats came after the Pentagon said the U.S. military had boarded a second tanker carrying oil from Iran. Both sides are try...

item.title: U.S. Seizes Second Tanker Carrying Iranian Oil
item.description: Navy destroyers are also shadowing several other vessels that left from an Iranian port before the U.S.-imposed blockade began, a U.S. military offici...

item.title: Zelensky details deals Ukraine signed to bolster Gulf nations’ defense against Iranian drones.
